title: NASA Satellite: Crash Window
objective: Memorize and replay the satellite s pulse commands to keep it aligned for safe breakup, surviving longer waves as the descent speeds up, errors stack heat, and one final mistake on a full heat bar means impact.

controls.input: Tap the glowing thruster pads in the order they flashed
controls.outcome: The satellite corrects course, your combo rises, and the next wave gets longer and faster.

controls.input: Press and hold the obscured pad for a brief beat
controls.outcome: You reveal the hidden pulse safely, but your score multiplier pauses during the scan.

controls.input: Tap the center NASA uplink button between rounds
controls.outcome: You bank the current combo into score and vent a small amount of heat before the next sequence.

steps: A short pulse pattern plays across 3 to 5 thruster pads with sharp synth beeps.
You replay the pattern from memory before the crash timer ring closes.
Each clear adds a beat, speeds playback, and pushes the satellite closer to Earth on the background descent track.
Every few rounds, the satellite rolls into Earth s shadow and one pulse becomes hidden unless you spend time scanning it.
Mistakes add heat; filling the heat meter or missing the timer triggers atmospheric breakup and ends the run.
scoring: Score 100 points per correct input, plus a rising combo multiplier for flawless rounds. Hidden dark-side pulses cleared without scanning give bonus points. Tapping Emergency Stabilize banks your multiplier into score, so
tips: Bank your combo before a shadow round if the pattern is already long.
Use scan only when the hidden pulse position is unclear; blind clears are the biggest score spike.
Listen for pitch as well as light because each pad has its own audio cue.
Short sessions come from high speed escalation: three mistakes or one overheated round can end a run fast.
